title: Chapter Fifteen: First Blood
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

They rode hard.

The four companions left Xiangyang on horseback and drove northwest at a pace that silenced Heon Won-chang, who had expected a leisurely tour of the martial world. Post stations lay open to them -- the privilege of Desolate Sect -- and their qi-enhanced endurance kept them in the saddle far longer than any ordinary rider could manage.

No room for leisure. No idle sightseeing. This was how the Desolate Sect operated.

But there was no post road from Huguang to Shaanxi. They moved at the horses' pace, preserving the animals' stamina through mountain passes and river crossings. The landscape changed as they rode northwest -- the lush river valleys of Huguang giving way to drier hills, terraced fields clinging to slopes, the air growing thinner and colder with altitude.

Yeon-shin used the days well. He practised the Charged Rotational Strike in stolen moments at each camp, feeling the chained force grow more natural with every repetition. At night, while Heon Won-chang slept and the two blue-rank warriors kept a loose perimeter, he sat cross-legged and circulated his newly strengthened true energy through the meridians, mapping its limits.

The burning at his crown came and went. Some mornings it was barely there. Others, it pulsed behind his eyes until his vision greyed at the edges. He breathed through it. Pushed it down.

*Not yet. There is work to do first.*

Ten days. Xiangyang to Zhenping County.

"Phew."

Heon Won-chang dismounted weakly. More mental than physical. Baek Mi-ryeo clicked her tongue.

"You cannot even imagine what happens when martial artists use the actual post roads."

Heon Won-chang went pale.

The county magistrate came out to meet them. Yeon-shin studied him. Round-faced. Well-fed. The look of a man who had not missed many meals despite a Blood Flame Cult member ruining his county's crops.

The magistrate's expression held relief and irritation in equal measure.

"How long has it been since I sent my urgent message? How frustrating! Get down! I was going to show respect to the swords of the Great Ming Empire, but to think you would be so leisurely! Can you even catch that person with just you people?"

Yeon-shin looked at Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo. Their faces showed familiar indifference. They had dealt with this kind before.

"Among officials," Cheong Myeong said mildly, tapping the back of his hood, "there are some who do not know any better. They think Desolate Sect is their broom. Only when it is their first time. Many county officials have never seen a real martial artist."

"What! You bastard! What rudeness --"

"Should I press your mute acupoint?"

Before Cheong Myeong could finish his sentence, the official's voice was cut short.

Yeon-shin's hand, filled with true energy, pressed between the spine and skull. Taller than the magistrate, he looked down with indifferent eyes.

"I do not take orders from you."

Sweat beaded on the man's forehead. His body trembled. Yeon-shin released the pressure point and turned.

Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo were looking at him with satisfaction.

"Sir Jeong, you have learned acupoint techniques as well?" Heon Won-chang exclaimed. "My goodness."

For Yeon-shin, who had mastered the human body through the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique, acupoint pressing was a natural study. The delicate stimulation of another person's meridians required knowledge he already possessed.

"You know the saying that officials cannot be touched?" Cheong Myeong put his arm around Yeon-shin's shoulder. "We are martial artists freer than any other when it comes to officials. The imperial court allowed it. Idiots who think we are wearing shackles can be handled exactly like that."

"Still, you should not overdo it," Baek Mi-ryeo said from beside them. Then, in a smaller voice: "That was well done."

"Aye."

The awkwardness between them had been thinning since the sparring match. The ten days of travel had ground more of it away. Now Baek Mi-ryeo's quiet praise settled the last of it.

Yeon-shin's eyes narrowed. He was studying the terrain below the government office -- farmland, livestock pens, houses, and beyond them, green hills rising to a mountain range. Lush undergrowth. Good country for hiding.

"Over there," he said. "Below the village."

"Hm?"

"Blood Flame Cult member."

He kicked the ground and sprinted.

---

The wind tore past his ears. True energy surged through his body.

*I will cut him in one stroke.*

Below, a commotion in the village fields. Villagers shouting. A red-haired man laughing at them, telling them they should not speak that way.

They had not noticed the four warriors descending from the hill. The distance was still great.

A rush of air beside his cheek. The cloth of a hood brushing past.

Cheong Myeong overtook him with impossible lightness -- feet barely touching the grass, body gliding like a leaf on the wind. The realm of the Elf Clan. Walking on grass tips without touching the ground.

*Damn it.*

Baek Mi-ryeo passed him next, her footwork light and precise. Then Heon Won-chang, panting, saying he would go on ahead.

Yeon-shin's lightness skills were the weakest part of his repertoire. He knew it. He had been trying to develop movement techniques, but the inspiration had not come.

His eyes moved. Farmland. Houses. Green hills. The lush undergrowth leading to the mountain range.

*He comes periodically. He lives nearby. Red hair cannot be hidden easily. Blood Flame doctrine forbids covering the head.*

The Blood Flame Cult member blocked Cheong Myeong's first strike and was sent tumbling backward. Not at the level to handle a Desolate Sect blue-rank.

But the man was not fighting to win.

Yeon-shin changed direction. Ran for the hills.

"Just in case."

By the time he reached the path entering the mountain, he knew he had chosen correctly.

Red hair, streaming behind him. A man in a bright red martial uniform, running for his life, having shaken off both Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo.

"Out of the way!"

Yeon-shin spread all five fingers and gripped the hilt. Radiant Demon Formation drew the sword path. He settled into the Horseback Stance -- right foot forward, upper body low, qi rolling off him in waves.

His Quick Sword was something even Radiant Demon Wing's blue-rank experts found difficult to receive.

The Blood Flame man's demonic expression shifted. Surprise. Recognition.

"A greenhorn? No. That is something else --"

He changed direction. Ran wide.

*What?*

The man fled in a great arc around him, not even attempting to engage.

Yeon-shin stood in his perfect stance and watched the red-haired man sprint away.

*Every experience I have -- every duel, every spar -- assumed the enemy would face me. This is different. This is the real martial world, where survival matters more than honour, where flight is a weapon as sharp as any blade.*

Apart from his aptitude for martial arts, he discovered in himself the naivety that all young warriors carry into their first mission. It was a bitter taste.

His first step into the real martial world.

He did not have the lightness skills to catch the man. He could only watch the receding figure disappear into the undergrowth.

"What a blunder."

Cheong Myeong appeared at his shoulder, smiling bitterly.

"Do not blame yourself. It is our fault."

"He had the Nine Lives Grass," Baek Mi-ryeo said, arriving behind them, breathing hard. "A divine art for escaping. Fitting for a Blood Flame remnant."

*A movement technique faster than the Elf Clan's lightness skill. From a man who has not been in the Blood Flame Cult long.*

"Those bloody cultists gain power through sorcery," Cheong Myeong explained. "They burn the blood of others and themselves together to refine martial arts in a hurry. At the cost of their lifespan. His lightness skill outstripped his swordsmanship."

"Acting alone for this long is strange," Baek Mi-ryeo said. "Blood Flame Cult members are not free spirits. There is something more."

Heon Won-chang arrived, gasping. Baek Mi-ryeo turned to him.

"Your overall martial arts achievement is adequate. Befitting the white rank. But your stamina cannot keep up. In the martial world, you will never fight an opponent in perfect condition. It is lacking."

He apologised. Bowed.

She turned to Yeon-shin. Her eyes softened.

"The foundation is astonishing. It is to the extent that I wonder if such cases exist even among the Nine Sects. I realised it watching you run without any technique. Just looking at the body -- no one in Radiant Demon Wing could keep pace. Even our sneaky Elf Clan friend."

"That is right," Cheong Myeong said, tapping Yeon-shin's shoulder. "There is no need to be discouraged about lightness skills now."

*The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique. All those years.*

"Is it alright for us to stay at the government office?" Yeon-shin asked. "I was a bit harsh."

"What will you do if it is not? This is Desolate Sect. That magistrate should cooperate."

Cheong Myeong agreed. It was, to them, a trivial matter.

But Yeon-shin remembered the prestige of the county magistrate in Xinye -- how the office had rivalled even the Jeong family. What he had done today would have been unthinkable six months ago.

*I really have joined a comfortable place. I just need to do well.*

"By the way," Cheong Myeong said. "Do you want to learn how to walk?"

"Walking techniques? Divine arts?"

Cheong Myeong scratched his hood with visible reluctance.

"I cannot pass down my clan's martial arts. But I can teach you how the Elf Clan moves. Most people cannot do it even if they know. But I think you can."

Dust rose at Cheong Myeong's feet. Without any flow of true energy, the hem of his trousers fluttered in a single gust of wind.

"Look."

He kicked off the ground and soared -- stepping on the side of a tree, once, twice, three times, then standing upright on a branch at the top. Not a sound. Not a single sound. It was not human movement. It was something older, something that belonged to the forests and the wind.

The Desolate Sect warriors who had unknowingly drifted closer stood with their mouths open, heads tilted back, watching.

Cheong Myeong closed his eyes. Inhaled the forest air. Then dropped from the branch like a feather, landing with a touch so light the dust barely stirred.

A divine walking technique that was not merely surprising but frightening. No trace in one's movements. No sound. It was like having a fatal move that the enemy could never anticipate.

*It is not true energy. But something like true energy supported the body. The natural force of the world itself, channelled through instinct rather than cultivation.*

Yeon-shin stared. Recalled the movements. Fell into thought.

The burning at the crown of his head pulsed faintly. His vision wavered, then steadied.

*Not now.*

"Perhaps... you understand?" Cheong Myeong landed like a falling leaf. His expression was disbelieving.

"I was not meditating. I was pondering how to create wind with true energy. Since I do not have the same power as the Elf Clan..."

"You gained insight after seeing the energy of nature once? How --"

"Is that not why you showed it?" Yeon-shin said. "A few ideas came to mind. If I segment the true energy by breaking down the movements into forms..."

His focus sharpened. The trance was pulling at him -- the edge of something enormous, a new technique taking shape in the architecture of his mind.

He blinked. Smiled.

"Thank you for the excellent inspiration."

Cheong Myeong stared at him. Heon Won-chang arrived with the same expression.

"Shall we unpack at our lodgings?" Won-chang suggested gently.

"Aye. Let us do that."

---

They settled in the government office. Yeon-shin released the magistrate's blocked acupoints and ignored the man's fearful glances. He had more important things to think about.

He pondered martial arts until he fell asleep. Pondered them in his dreams.

*If I think about the movement in advance and emit true energy in short bursts at each joint of movement...*

His eyes closed. The burning at his crown settled into a low hum, the clock of his shortened life ticking steadily behind the door he kept shut.

---

Morning.

Cheong Myeong woke in his hemp hammock strung between two trees, sunlight warm on his face. He blinked, smiled, and looked down.

A boy stood in the clearing below. Eyes closed. The Desolate Sword at his waist. His body surrounded by a quiet field of energy -- not hidden, not guarded. The boldness of not caring who watched.

Cheong Myeong was about to smile.

Then Yeon-shin moved.

A slight shake, catching the morning sun. Dust rose at the tip of his foot. He spun, kicked the ground, and the scattering leaves were captured in his movements -- drawn into the wake of something that should not have been possible for a human to learn overnight.

Cheong Myeong's eyes went wide.

"Insane."

The word left him before he could stop it. Below, the boy continued to move through the clearing, and the leaves danced around him as if the wind itself had chosen to follow.
